summary: This topic details the process of mapping defined subjects to specific classes, streams, and sections. It includes selecting the class, adding subjects, and defining the subject type (General, Optional, Additional, Vocational) which impacts how subjects are assigned to students.
navigation: Subject Class Mapping (or Subject Course Mapping)
steps: Navigate to the 'Academics' section via the 'Settings' icon.
Go to 'Subject Class Mapping' (or 'Subject Course Mapping').
Select the 'Class' for which you are creating the mapping (e.g., Class 5th).
Optionally, select multiple classes if they share common subjects.
The 'Stream' and 'Section' will be selected by default or can be specified.
Click to 'Add Detail of the Subject' (or similar option).
Select the 'Subject' from the dropdown list (this list contains subjects previously defined).
The 'Assessment Model' will automatically populate based on the subject definition.
Select the 'Subject Type' (e.g., General, Optional, Additional, Vocational).
Click 'Add' to map the subject to the class.
Repeat the process to add all relevant subjects for the selected class.
business_rules: If a subject is already mapped to a class, its original name cannot be changed from the subject definition; only its 'Display Name' can be edited within the mapping.
Subjects marked as 'General' are applied to all students in the class by default.
Subjects marked as 'Optional', 'Additional', or 'Vocational' are not applied to all students by default and require a separate assignment process for individual students.
important_notes: The 'Display Name' set in the mapping is what students will see.
You can map common subjects to multiple classes simultaneously.
questions: How do I map subjects to a specific class?
What is the difference between 'General' and 'Optional' subject types in class mapping?
Can I map the same subject to several classes at once?
Where can I change the name of a subject as it appears to students?
What happens if I don't define a subject type?
